previous_statement: (Dimension consequence). Since Φ is an affine isomorphism between F_{S(A)}(φ) and the set D = {T ∈ π_φ(A)'^+ : ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1}, we have dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = dim(D). The set D is a base (cross-section) of the cone π_φ(A)'^+ cut by the hyperplane ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1 in the real vector space π_φ(A)'^{sa}. Since ⟨·ξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ is a single real-linear constraint on π_φ(A)'^{sa}, we have dim(D) = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1 (the -1 is the codimension of the normalisation hyperplane within the affine span of D). This requires that ξ_φ is a separating vector for π_φ(A)', or at least that the functional T ↦ ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ is faithful on π_φ(A)'^+ (which follows from ξ_φ being cyclic for π_φ(A), hence separating for π_φ(A)' by Tomita-Takesaki theory, when π_φ(A)'' = π_φ(A)̄^{WOT}).
new_statement:
(Dimension consequence — amended). Since Φ is an affine isomorphism between F_{S(A)}(φ) and D = {T ∈ π_φ(A)'^+ : ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1}, we have dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = dim(D). We claim dim(D) = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1.

Let V = π_φ(A)'^{sa} and f : V → R the functional f(T) = ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩, which is real-linear. D = π_φ(A)'^+ ∩ f⁻¹(1), a cross-section of the positive cone by a hyperplane.

The formula dim(D) = dim_R(V) - 1 requires showing that aff(D) = f⁻¹(1), i.e., that the affine span of D fills the entire normalisation hyperplane. This follows from two facts:

(a) The cone π_φ(A)'^+ SPANS V (every self-adjoint operator is a difference of two positive operators: T = T₊ - T₋ where T₊, T₋ ≥ 0).

(b) The cone π_φ(A)'^+ has NONEMPTY INTERIOR in V: the identity I ∈ π_φ(A)' is a strictly positive element, so a ball around I in V lies in π_φ(A)'^+.

From (a) and (b): take T₀ = I/⟨ξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = I (since ξ_φ is a unit vector), so f(T₀) = 1 and T₀ ∈ int(π_φ(A)'^+). For any S ∈ V with f(S) = 0 (i.e., S ∈ ker(f)), T₀ + εS ∈ π_φ(A)'^+ for sufficiently small ε > 0 (since T₀ is interior), and f(T₀ + εS) = 1. Hence T₀ + εS ∈ D, showing ker(f) ⊆ {directions of D from T₀}, so aff(D) = f⁻¹(1) and dim(D) = dim_R(V) - 1.

Note: Faithfulness of f on π_φ(A)'^+ (equivalently, ξ_φ being separating for π_φ(A)') is NOT required for this dimension formula — it suffices that f is a non-zero linear functional on V and the cone has interior. Faithfulness is used elsewhere (injectivity, Node 1.1.4).

Combining with Nodes 1.1.1–1.1.5: dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = dim(D) = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1. This is a deduction depending on Nodes 1.1.1–1.1.5.
